what: Use as_forest_data() to standardize a coefficient table
title: Prepare Forest Data with Helper Functions
text: as_forest_data() converts column names internal structure used ggforestplotR. result contains columns expected ggforestplot(), add_forest_table(), add_split_table(). data standardized, can pass straight ggforestplot().
code: raw_coefs <- data.frame(   variable = c("Age", "BMI", "Treatment"),   beta = c(0.10, -0.08, 0.34),   lower = c(0.02, -0.16, 0.12),   upper = c(0.18, 0.00, 0.56),   display = c("Age", "BMI", "Treatment"),   section = c("Clinical", "Clinical", "Treatment"),   sample_size = c(120, 115, 98),   p_value = c(0.04, 0.15, 0.001) )  forest_ready <- as_forest_data(   data = raw_coefs,   term = "variable",   estimate = "beta",   conf.low = "lower",   conf.high = "upper",   label = "display",   grouping = "section",   n = "sample_size",   p.value = "p_value" ) ggforestplot(forest_ready)

title: Prepare Forest Data with Helper Functions
text: broom available, tidy_forest_model() can pull coefficient estimates confidence limits fitted model. returned object can passed directly ggforestplot().
code: fit <- lm(mpg ~ wt + hp + qsec, data = mtcars)  model_ready <- tidy_forest_model(fit) ggforestplot(model_ready)

title: Get Started with ggforestplotR
text: Use package one three ways: Start coefficient table map required columns directly. Start fitted model let tidy_forest_model() ggforestplot() call broom::tidy(). Add add_forest_table() add_split_table() styling main plot. article covers basic entry points minimum data need.

title: Get Started with ggforestplotR
text: simplest input data frame term, estimate, confidence limits. columns use different names, map explicitly.
code: basic_coefs <- data.frame(   term = c("Age", "BMI", "Treatment"),   estimate = c(0.10, -0.08, 0.34),   conf.low = c(0.02, -0.16, 0.12),   conf.high = c(0.18, 0.00, 0.56) )  ggforestplot(basic_coefs)

what: Add grouped sections and row striping
title: Get Started with ggforestplotR
text: Use grouping want related variables separated labeled panels. Add striped_rows = TRUE color alternating rows plot.
code: sectioned_coefs <- data.frame(   term = c("Age", "BMI", "Smoking", "Stage II", "Stage III", "Nodes"),   estimate = c(0.10, -0.08, 0.20, 0.34, 0.52, 0.28),   conf.low = c(0.02, -0.16, 0.05, 0.12, 0.20, 0.06),   conf.high = c(0.18, 0.00, 0.35, 0.56, 0.84, 0.50),   section = c("Clinical", "Clinical", "Clinical", "Tumor", "Tumor", "Tumor") )  ggforestplot(   sectioned_coefs,   grouping = "section",   striped_rows = TRUE,   stripe_fill = "grey94" )

title: Get Started with ggforestplotR
text: Use add_forest_table() add summary table forest plot.
code: tabled_coefs <- data.frame(   term = c("Age", "BMI", "Smoking", "Stage II", "Stage III"),   estimate = c(0.12, -0.10, 0.18, 0.30, 0.46),   conf.low = c(0.03, -0.18, 0.04, 0.10, 0.18),   conf.high = c(0.21, -0.02, 0.32, 0.50, 0.74),   sample_size = c(120, 115, 98, 87, 83) )  ggforestplot(tabled_coefs, n = "sample_size", striped_rows = TRUE) +   add_forest_table(     position = "left",     show_n = TRUE,     estimate_label = "Beta"   )

title: Get Started with ggforestplotR
text: Use add_split_table() create traditional looking forest plot, summary data either side plot.
code: ggforestplot(tabled_coefs, n = "sample_size", striped_rows = T) +   add_split_table(     left_columns = c("term", "n"),     right_columns = c("estimate")   )

title: Get Started with ggforestplotR
text: broom installed, ggforestplot() can work directly fitted model.
code: fit <- lm(mpg ~ wt + hp + qsec, data = mtcars)  ggforestplot(fit, sort_terms = "descending")

title: Get Started with ggforestplotR
text: detail, see: ggforestplotR-plot-customization enhanced customization plots summary tables. ggforestplotR-data-helpers as_forest_data() tidy_forest_model().

title: Customize Forest Plots and Tables
code: coefs <- data.frame(   term = c("Age", "BMI", "Smoking", "Stage II", "Stage III"),   estimate = c(0.12, -0.10, 0.18, 0.30, 0.46),   conf.low = c(0.03, -0.18, 0.04, 0.10, 0.18),   conf.high = c(0.21, -0.02, 0.32, 0.50, 0.74),   sample_size = c(120, 115, 98, 87, 83),   p_value = c(0.04, 0.15, 0.29, 0.001, 0.75),   section = c("Clinical", "Clinical", "Clinical", "Tumor", "Tumor") )

what: Group rows and control strip placement
title: Customize Forest Plots and Tables
text: grouping creates section panels, grouping_strip_position controls side gets strip labels.
code: ggforestplot(   coefs,   grouping = "section",   grouping_strip_position = "right",   striped_rows = TRUE )

title: Customize Forest Plots and Tables
text: Use separate_groups separate_lines want distinct visual separation variables. especially useful categorical variables many levels. separate_groups automatically appends variable name level.
code: block_coefs <- data.frame(   term = c("race_black", "race_white", "race_other", "age", "bmi"),   label = c("Black", "White", "Other", "Age", "BMI"),   estimate = c(0.24, 0.08, -0.04, 0.12, -0.09),   conf.low = c(0.10, -0.04, -0.18, 0.03, -0.17),   conf.high = c(0.38, 0.20, 0.10, 0.21, -0.01),   variable_block = c("Race", "Race", "Race", "Age", "BMI") )  ggforestplot(   block_coefs,   label = "label",   separate_groups = "variable_block",   separate_lines = TRUE,   striped_rows = TRUE ) +   scale_y_discrete(limits = rev(c("BMI", "Age", "Race: White",                                    "Race: Black", "Race: Other"))) #> Scale for y is already present. #> Adding another scale for y, which will replace the existing scale.

title: Customize Forest Plots and Tables
text: add_forest_table() allows attach model information coefficient plot. table can added either left right side allows customization. always add table LAST, styling plot function calls patchwork internally. patchwork requires specific syntax customize plots generally difficult get working correctly.
code: ggforestplot(   coefs,   grouping = "section",   grouping_strip_position = "right",   n = "sample_size",   p.value = "p_value",   striped_rows = TRUE ) +   add_forest_table(     show_n = TRUE,     show_p = TRUE,     estimate_label = "Beta"   )

title: Customize Forest Plots and Tables
text: add_forest_table also lets change minor styling elements forest table.
code: ggforestplot(   coefs,   n = "sample_size",   p.value = "p_value",   striped_rows = TRUE ) +   add_forest_table(     position = "left",     show_n = TRUE,     show_p = TRUE,     estimate_label = "Beta",     grid_lines = T,     grid_line_linetype = 2,     grid_line_colour = "red"   )

title: Customize Forest Plots and Tables
text: add_split_table() can used create traditional looking forest plots. can choose summary information goes side. Like add_forest_table(), added plot-level styling.
code: ggforestplot(   coefs,   n = "sample_size",   p.value = "p_value",   striped_rows = TRUE ) +   scale_x_continuous(limits = c(-.8,.8)) +   add_split_table(     left_columns = c("term","n"),     right_columns = c("estimate","p"),     estimate_label = "Beta"   )

what: Plotting other types of model coefficients
title: Customize Forest Plots and Tables
text: can use exponentiate = TRUE models log-odds scale (similar).  can survival models well.
code: data(CO2)  l1 <- glm(Treatment ~ conc + uptake + Type, family = binomial(link = "logit"),      data = CO2) ggforestplot(l1, exponentiate = TRUE, striped_rows = T) +   add_forest_table(position = "left",                     estimate_label = "OR",                     show_p = F) lung <- survival::lung  lung <- lung |>     dplyr::mutate(     status = dplyr::recode(status, `1` = 0, `2` = 1)   )  s1 <- survival::coxph(Surv(time, status) ~ sex + age + ph.karno + pat.karno, data = lung) ggforestplot(s1, exponentiate = T, striped_rows = T) +   add_forest_table(estimate_label = "HR")

title: Customize Forest Plots and Tables
text: group argument handy comparing estimates several models.
code: comparison_coefs <- data.frame(   term = rep(c("Age", "BMI", "Smoking", "Stage II", "Stage III"), 2),   estimate = c(0.12, -0.10, 0.18, 0.30, 0.46, 0.08, -0.05, 0.24, 0.40, 0.58),   conf.low = c(0.03, -0.18, 0.04, 0.10, 0.18, 0.00, -0.13, 0.10, 0.20, 0.30),   conf.high = c(0.21, -0.02, 0.32, 0.50, 0.74, 0.16, 0.03, 0.38, 0.60, 0.86),   model = rep(c("Model A", "Model B"), each = 5),   section = rep(c("Clinical", "Clinical", "Clinical", "Tumor", "Tumor"), 2) )  ggforestplot(   comparison_coefs,   group = "model",   grouping = "section",   striped_rows = TRUE,   dodge_width = 0.5,   grouping_strip_position = "right" ) +   theme(legend.position = "bottom")

title: Authors and Citation
text: Carson Richardson. Author, maintainer, copyright holder.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Richardson C (2026). ggforestplotR: Publication-Ready Forest Plots 'ggplot2'. R package version 0.1.1, https://thatoneguy006.github.io/ggforestplotR/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {ggforestplotR: Publication-Ready Forest Plots with 'ggplot2'},   author = {Carson Richardson},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.1},   url = {https://thatoneguy006.github.io/ggforestplotR/}, }

title: ggforestplotR
text: ggforestplotR provides ggplot2-first workflow building forest plots tidy coefficient tables fitted model objects.

title: ggforestplotR
code: install.packages("ggforestplotR")

title: ggforestplotR
code: #install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("thatoneguy006/ggforestplotR")

title: ggforestplotR
text: ggforestplotR currently supports two core workflows: Plot directly table coefficient data. Plot using data fitted model object.

title: ggforestplotR
code: library(ggforestplotR) library(ggplot2)  sectioned_coefs <- data.frame(   term = c("Age", "BMI", "Smoking", "Stage II", "Stage III", "Nodes"),   estimate = c(0.10, -0.08, 0.20, 0.34, 0.52, 0.28),   conf.low = c(0.02, -0.16, 0.05, 0.12, 0.20, 0.06),   conf.high = c(0.18, 0.00, 0.35, 0.56, 0.84, 0.50),   section = c("Clinical", "Clinical", "Clinical", "Tumor", "Tumor", "Tumor") )  ggforestplot(   sectioned_coefs,   grouping = "section",   striped_rows = TRUE,   stripe_fill = "grey94",   grouping_strip_position = "right" )

title: ggforestplotR
code: ggforestplot(   sectioned_coefs,   striped_rows = TRUE,   stripe_fill = "grey94" ) +   add_forest_table()

title: ggforestplotR
code: ggforestplot(   sectioned_coefs,   striped_rows = TRUE,   stripe_fill = "grey94" ) +   add_split_table()

title: ggforestplotR
text: Get started: https://thatoneguy006.github.io/ggforestplotR/articles/ggforestplotR-get-started.html Plot & Table customization: https://thatoneguy006.github.io/ggforestplotR/articles/ggforestplotR-plot-customization.html Data helpers: https://thatoneguy006.github.io/ggforestplotR/articles/ggforestplotR-data-helpers.html

title: ggforestplotR
text: ggforestplot() builds plotting panel data frame supported model object. add_forest_table() attaches summary table left right side plot. add_split_table() creates traditional forestplot layout table columns sides plot. as_forest_data() standardizes custom coefficient data. tidy_forest_model() converts fitted models plotting-ready coefficient data.

what: Add a summary table to a forest plot — add_forest_table
title: Add a summary table to a forest plot — add_forest_table
text: Compose summary table onto forest plot.

title: Add a summary table to a forest plot — add_forest_table
code: add_forest_table(   plot = NULL,   position = c("left", "right"),   show_terms = TRUE,   show_n = NULL,   show_events = NULL,   show_estimate = TRUE,   show_p = FALSE,   columns = NULL,   term_header = "Term",   n_header = "N",   events_header = "Events",   estimate_label = "Estimate",   p_header = "P-value",   digits = NULL,   text_size = NULL,   header_text_size = NULL,   header_fontface = "bold",   header_family = NULL,   striped_rows = NULL,   stripe_fill = NULL,   stripe_colour = NULL,   grid_lines = FALSE,   grid_line_colour = "black",   grid_line_size = 0.3,   grid_line_linetype = 1 )

title: Add a summary table to a forest plot — add_forest_table
text: plot plot created ggforestplot(). Leave NULL use + add_forest_table(...) syntax. position Whether place table left right forest plot. show_terms Whether show term column table. show_n Whether show N column. Defaults TRUE underlying plot data includes n column. show_events Whether show Events column. Defaults TRUE underlying plot data includes events column. show_estimate Whether show formatted estimate confidence interval column. show_p Whether display p-value column. columns Optional explicit columns display side table, order appear. Accepts names "n", "events", "term", positions 1:5 corresponding term, n, events, estimate, p. supplied, overrides default show_* column selection. term_header Header text term column. n_header Header text N column. events_header Header text Events column. estimate_label Header label estimate column. p_header Header text p-value column. digits Number digits used formatting estimates p-values. Defaults 2. text_size Text size table contents. Defaults 3.2. header_text_size Header text size table column labels. Defaults 11. header_fontface Font face used table column labels. Defaults "bold". header_family Optional font family used table column labels. striped_rows Whether draw alternating row stripes behind table. Defaults stripe setting used ggforestplot(). stripe_fill Fill colour used striped rows. Defaults stripe fill used ggforestplot(). stripe_colour Outline colour striped rows. Defaults stripe outline used ggforestplot(). grid_lines Whether draw black horizontal grid lines table. grid_line_colour Colour used table grid lines. grid_line_size Line width used table grid lines. grid_line_linetype Line type used table grid lines.

title: Add a summary table to a forest plot — add_forest_table
text: patchwork-composed plot containing forest plot side table, ggplot add-object plot = NULL.

title: Add a summary table to a forest plot — add_forest_table
code: coefs <- data.frame(   term = c("Age", "BMI", "Treatment"),   estimate = c(0.3, -0.2, 0.4),   conf.low = c(0.1, -0.4, 0.2),   conf.high = c(0.5, 0.0, 0.6),   sample_size = c(120, 115, 98),   p_value = c(0.012, 0.031, 0.004) )  p <- ggforestplot(coefs, n = "sample_size", p.value = "p_value") add_forest_table(   p,   position = "left",   show_n = TRUE,   show_p = TRUE,   estimate_label = "Beta" )   ggforestplot(coefs, n = "sample_size", p.value = "p_value") +   add_forest_table(     position = "right",     show_n = TRUE,     show_p = TRUE,     estimate_label = "Beta"   )

what: Add split tables around a forest plot — add_split_table
title: Add split tables around a forest plot — add_split_table
text: Compose split table blocks around forest plot summary data appear sides plotting panel.

title: Add split tables around a forest plot — add_split_table
code: add_split_table(   plot = NULL,   show_terms = TRUE,   show_n = NULL,   show_events = NULL,   show_estimate = TRUE,   show_p = FALSE,   left_columns = NULL,   right_columns = NULL,   term_header = "Term",   n_header = "N",   events_header = "Events",   estimate_label = "Estimate",   p_header = "P-value",   digits = NULL,   text_size = NULL,   header_text_size = NULL,   header_fontface = "bold",   header_family = NULL,   striped_rows = NULL,   stripe_fill = NULL,   stripe_colour = NULL,   left_width = NULL,   plot_width = NULL,   right_width = NULL )

title: Add split tables around a forest plot — add_split_table
text: plot plot created ggforestplot(). Leave NULL use + add_split_table(...) syntax. show_terms Whether include term column default left-side selection left_columns supplied. show_n Whether include N column default left-side selection left_columns supplied. Defaults TRUE underlying plot data include n column. show_events Whether include Events column default left-side selection left_columns supplied. Defaults TRUE underlying plot data include events column. show_estimate Whether include formatted estimate confidence interval column default right-side selection right_columns supplied. show_p Whether include p-value column default right-side selection right_columns supplied. left_columns Optional explicit columns place left side forest plot. Accepts names "term", "n", "events", positions 1:5 corresponding term, n, events, estimate, p. right_columns Optional explicit columns place right side forest plot. Accepts names "estimate" "p", positions 1:5 corresponding term, n, events, estimate, p. term_header Header text term column. n_header Header text N column. events_header Header text Events column. estimate_label Header label estimate column. p_header Header text p-value column. digits Number digits used formatting estimates p-values. Defaults 2. text_size Text size table contents. Defaults 3.2. header_text_size Header text size table column labels. Defaults 11. header_fontface Font face used table column labels. Defaults "bold". header_family Optional font family used table column labels. striped_rows Whether draw alternating row stripes behind split table layout. Defaults stripe setting used ggforestplot(). stripe_fill Fill colour used striped rows. Defaults stripe fill used ggforestplot(). stripe_colour Outline colour striped rows. Defaults stripe outline used ggforestplot(). left_width Optional width allocated left table block. default derived number displayed left-side columns relative plot_width. plot_width Optional width allocated forest plot panel. Defaults 2.5. right_width Optional width allocated right table block. default derived number displayed right-side columns relative plot_width.

title: Add split tables around a forest plot — add_split_table
text: patchwork-composed plot containing left table, forest plot, right table, ggplot add-object plot = NULL.

title: Add split tables around a forest plot — add_split_table
code: coefs <- data.frame(   term = c("Age", "BMI", "Treatment"),   estimate = c(0.3, -0.2, 0.4),   conf.low = c(0.1, -0.4, 0.2),   conf.high = c(0.5, 0.0, 0.6),   sample_size = c(120, 115, 98),   p_value = c(0.012, 0.031, 0.004) )  p <- ggforestplot(coefs, n = "sample_size", p.value = "p_value") add_split_table(   p,   left_columns = c("term", "n"),   right_columns = c("estimate", "p"),   estimate_label = "HR" )   ggforestplot(coefs, n = "sample_size", p.value = "p_value") +   add_split_table(     left_columns = c(1, 2),     right_columns = c(4, 5),     estimate_label = "HR"   )

what: Standardize coefficient data for forest plots — as_forest_data
title: Standardize coefficient data for forest plots — as_forest_data
text: Standardizes coefficient table internal forest-plot data structure used throughout ggforestplotR.

title: Standardize coefficient data for forest plots — as_forest_data
code: as_forest_data(   data,   term,   estimate,   conf.low,   conf.high,   label = term,   group = NULL,   grouping = NULL,   separate_groups = NULL,   n = NULL,   events = NULL,   p.value = NULL,   exponentiate = FALSE,   sort_terms = c("none", "descending", "ascending") )

title: Standardize coefficient data for forest plots — as_forest_data
text: data data frame containing coefficient estimates intervals. term Column name holding model term identifier. estimate Column name holding point estimate. conf.low Column name holding lower confidence bound. conf.high Column name holding upper confidence bound. label Optional column name used displayed row label. group Optional column name used color-grouping multiple estimates per row. grouping Optional column name used split rows grouped plot sections. separate_groups Optional column name used identify labeled variable blocks can outlined separator lines. n Optional column name holding sample sizes N labels table helpers. events Optional column name holding event counts event labels table helpers. p.value Optional column name holding p-values. exponentiate Logical; TRUE, require positive values estimates intervals. sort_terms sort rows: "none", "descending", "ascending".

title: Standardize coefficient data for forest plots — as_forest_data
text: standardized data frame ready ggforestplot() table composition helpers.

title: Standardize coefficient data for forest plots — as_forest_data
code: raw <- data.frame(   variable = c("Age", "BMI", "Treatment"),   beta = c(0.10, -0.08, 0.34),   lower = c(0.02, -0.16, 0.12),   upper = c(0.18, 0.00, 0.56) )  as_forest_data(   data = raw,   term = "variable",   estimate = "beta",   conf.low = "lower",   conf.high = "upper" ) #>        term estimate conf.low conf.high     label group grouping #> 1       Age     0.10     0.02      0.18       Age  <NA>     <NA> #> 2       BMI    -0.08    -0.16      0.00       BMI  <NA>     <NA> #> 3 Treatment     0.34     0.12      0.56 Treatment  <NA>     <NA> #>   separate_groups    n events p.value #> 1            <NA> <NA>   <NA>      NA #> 2            <NA> <NA>   <NA>      NA #> 3            <NA> <NA>   <NA>      NA

what: Draw a ggplot2 forest plot — ggforestplot
title: Draw a ggplot2 forest plot — ggforestplot
text: Builds forest plot standardized coefficient data directly fitted model.

title: Draw a ggplot2 forest plot — ggforestplot
code: ggforestplot(   data,   term = "term",   estimate = "estimate",   conf.low = "conf.low",   conf.high = "conf.high",   label = term,   group = NULL,   grouping = NULL,   grouping_strip_position = c("left", "right"),   separate_groups = NULL,   n = NULL,   events = NULL,   p.value = NULL,   exponentiate = FALSE,   sort_terms = c("none", "descending", "ascending"),   point_size = 2.3,   point_shape = 19,   line_size = 0.5,   staple_width = 0.2,   dodge_width = 0.6,   separate_lines = FALSE,   separator_line_linetype = 2,   separator_line_colour = "black",   separator_line_size = 0.4,   striped_rows = FALSE,   stripe_fill = "grey95",   stripe_colour = NA,   zero_line = TRUE,   zero_line_linetype = 2,   zero_line_colour = "grey60" )

title: Draw a ggplot2 forest plot — ggforestplot
text: data Either tidy coefficient data frame model object supported broom::tidy(). term Column name holding model term identifiers. estimate Column name holding point estimates. conf.low Column name holding lower confidence bounds. conf.high Column name holding upper confidence bounds. label Optional column name used displayed row labels. group Optional column name used color-grouping estimates. grouping Optional column name used split rows grouped plot sections. grouping_strip_position Positioning grouped section strips. separate_groups Optional column name used identify labeled variable blocks can outlined grid lines. n Optional column name holding sample sizes N labels table helpers. events Optional column name holding event counts event labels table helpers. p.value Optional column name holding p-values. exponentiate Logical; TRUE, transform estimates draw axis log scale null line 1. sort_terms sort rows: "none", "descending", "ascending". point_size Point size coefficient markers. point_shape Shape used coefficient markers. line_size Line width confidence intervals. staple_width Width terminal staples confidence interval lines. dodge_width Horizontal dodging used grouped estimates. separate_lines Logical; TRUE, draw grid lines around labeled block identified separate_groups. separator_line_linetype Line type used separator lines. separator_line_colour Colour used separator lines. separator_line_size Line width used separator lines. striped_rows Logical; TRUE, shade alternating rows. stripe_fill Fill color used shaded rows. stripe_colour Border color shaded rows. zero_line Logical; TRUE, draw null reference line. zero_line_linetype Line type null reference line. zero_line_colour Color null reference line.

title: Draw a ggplot2 forest plot — ggforestplot
text: ggplot object. Use standard ggplot2 functions ggplot2::labs() plot labels, add composition helpers styling main plot.

title: Draw a ggplot2 forest plot — ggforestplot
code: coefs <- data.frame(   term = c("Age", "BMI", "Treatment"),   estimate = c(0.10, -0.08, 0.34),   conf.low = c(0.02, -0.16, 0.12),   conf.high = c(0.18, 0.00, 0.56) )  ggforestplot(coefs)   ggforestplot(coefs, striped_rows = TRUE, point_shape = 17)

what: ggforestplotR: Forest plots from model coefficients with ggplot2 — ggforestplotR-package
title: ggforestplotR: Forest plots from model coefficients with ggplot2 — ggforestplotR-package
text: Transform model coefficients flexible forest plots using ggplot2. Provides helpers standardize coefficient data range modelling workflows render publication-ready coefficient plots consistent interface.

title: ggforestplotR: Forest plots from model coefficients with ggplot2 — ggforestplotR-package
text: Maintainer: Carson Richardson carson.richardson@outlook.com [copyright holder]

what: Tidy a model object for forest plotting — tidy_forest_model
title: Tidy a model object for forest plotting — tidy_forest_model
text: Uses broom::tidy() convert fitted model forest-plot data.

title: Tidy a model object for forest plotting — tidy_forest_model
code: tidy_forest_model(   model,   conf.int = TRUE,   conf.level = 0.95,   exponentiate = FALSE,   intercept = FALSE,   sort_terms = c("none", "descending", "ascending") )

title: Tidy a model object for forest plotting — tidy_forest_model
text: model fitted model object supported broom::tidy(). conf.int Logical; TRUE, request confidence intervals broom::tidy(). conf.level Confidence level intervals. exponentiate Logical; passed broom::tidy(). intercept Logical; FALSE, drop intercept term. sort_terms sort rows: "none", "descending", "ascending".

title: Tidy a model object for forest plotting — tidy_forest_model
text: standardized coefficient data frame ready ggforestplot().

title: Tidy a model object for forest plotting — tidy_forest_model
code: if (requireNamespace("broom", quietly = TRUE)) {   fit <- lm(mpg ~ wt + hp + qsec, data = mtcars)   tidy_forest_model(fit)    set.seed(123)   logit_data <- data.frame(     age = rnorm(250, mean = 62, sd = 8),     bmi = rnorm(250, mean = 28, sd = 4),     treatment = factor(rbinom(250, 1, 0.45), labels = c("Control", "Treatment"))   )   linpred <- -9 + 0.09 * logit_data$age + 0.11 * logit_data$bmi +     0.9 * (logit_data$treatment == "Treatment")   logit_data$event <- rbinom(250, 1, plogis(linpred))   logit_fit <- glm(event ~ age + bmi + treatment, data = logit_data, family = binomial())    tidy_forest_model(logit_fit, exponentiate = TRUE) } #>                 term estimate conf.low conf.high              label group #> 1                age 1.103890 1.061512  1.151411                age  <NA> #> 2                bmi 1.118858 1.043220  1.204389                bmi  <NA> #> 3 treatmentTreatment 3.133149 1.782915  5.613038 treatmentTreatment  <NA> #>   grouping separate_groups    n events      p.value #> 1     <NA>            <NA> <NA>   <NA> 1.719452e-06 #> 2     <NA>            <NA> <NA>   <NA> 2.093412e-03 #> 3     <NA>            <NA> <NA>   <NA> 9.087778e-05

title: ggforestplotR 0.1.1
text: Added events column support across ggforestplot(), as_forest_data(), add_forest_table(), add_split_table(). (Thanks @sritchie73 suggestion) Added table header styling controls font size, font face, font family table helpers. (Thanks @sritchie73 suggestion) Split table composition code dedicated source files simplify maintenance public API. Refined split-table examples documentation match expanded numeric column mapping.

title: ggforestplotR 0.1.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-03-25

title: ggforestplotR 0.1.0
text: Refined split-table layout logic, including dynamic panel widths column counts, alignment controls, cleaner stripe/background handling. add_forest_table() now allows custom column ordering via columns argument. Converted package documentation roxygen-generated NAMESPACE .Rd files. Reworked package documentation, including pkgdown configuration, README installation guidance, vignette set.

title: ggforestplotR 0.0.0.9000
text: Added grouped section support forest plots. Added add_forest_table() add_split_table() plot-table composition. Added various plot table customization options. Added package documentation, tests, getting-started vignette.
